---
title: "get_message(): more examples"
---
<br>

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
    error = TRUE, 
    collapse = FALSE,
    comment = "##"
)
```

```{r setup}
library(saferDev)
```
<br>

### Dataset

```{r}
char1 <- "wilcox.test(c(1,1,3), c(1, 2, 4), paired = TRUE)" # single character string of a command to evaluate -> warning message
char2 <- "message('ahah')" # idem -> standard message
char3 <- "NOTGOOD" # idem -> error message
char4 <- "mean(2)" # idem -> no message
```
<br>

### Dataset info
```{r}
char1
char2
char3
```
<br>

### Simple example
```{r}
# By default, get_message() returns the first error message of the evaluated string.
# Here no error message
get_message(data = char1)

# Here no error message
get_message(data = char2)

# Error message
get_message(data = char3)
```
<br>

### Argument <code>kind</code>
```{r}
# To get the first potential warning message
get_message(data = char1, kind = "warning")

# To get the first potential standart message (neither error nor warning)
get_message(data = char2, kind = "message")
```
<br>

### Argument <code>header</code>
```{r}
# To get the output message without header.
get_message(data = char3, header = FALSE)

# To get the output message with header (by default).
get_message(data = char3, header = TRUE) 
```
<br>

### Argument <code>print_no</code>
```{r}
# Print a message when no "kind" message returned.
get_message(data = char1, print_no = TRUE)

# Example with potential returned warning message.
get_message(data = char2, kind = "warning", print_no = TRUE) 
```
<br>

### Argument <code>text</code>
```{r}
# String added to the output message, even if nothing reported (when print_no = TRUE).
# Useful when used inside a function to name it.
get_message(data = char3, text = "IN MY EXECUTION") 
```
<br>


### Argument <code>env</code>
```{r}
# Evaluation only in the indicated environment.
get_message(data = char1, env = .GlobalEnv)
get_message(data = char1, kind = "warning", env = asNamespace("stats"))

```
<br>

### Argument <code>safer_check</code>
```{r}
get_message(data = char2, safer_check = TRUE) # with TRUE, checkings are performed before main code running
```
<br>

### Argument <code>lib_path</code> and <code>error_text</code>
```{r}
get_message(
    lib_path = ".", # absolute pathways of the directories containing the required packages if not in the default directories.
    error_text = "TEXT ADDED" # add information in error messages returned by the function.
)
```


### All the arguments
```{r}
get_message(
    data = char1, 
    kind = "warning", # to get the first warning message
    header = FALSE, # to get error messages (by default) without header
    print_no = TRUE, # print a message when no error reported
    text = "IN A", # single character string added to the output message (even if nothing reported)
    env = NULL,
    safer_check = TRUE, # Single logical value. Perform some "safer" checks (see https://github.com/safer-r)?
    lib_path = NULL, # absolute pathways of the directories containing the required packages if not in the default directories.
    error_text = "" # add information in error messages returned by the function.
) # print the warning message in text in the environment R_GlobalEnv
```
